page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 96 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

In benchmark testing, the m8gn.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory performance is also highly rated, with top-tier results in both cached small-block and uncached large-block read/write memory bandwidth tests. The instance excels in high-throughput operations, achieving top-tier performance in Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and database operations.

A key feature of this instance is its baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, making it highly suitable for network-intensive workloads. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached EBS volumes, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for storage-heavy tasks. Given its high multi-core efficiency and substantial network capacity, the m8gn.24xlarge is well-suited for large-scale web serving, database operations, software compilation, and medium-to-large scale LLM inference text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance powered by 96 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Redis SET operations. With a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, it is highly optimized for network-heavy workloads. However, the absence of local storage means all storage operations rely on network-attached EBS volumes. The m8gn.24xlarge is highly suited for high-throughput database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.24xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 96 dedicated Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An ARM64-based general-purpose instance featuring ninety-six dedicated cores and high-speed three-hundred gigabit network bandwidth for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
